repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Newspapers, Photographs, Printed Materials, Publications.
did.abstract: Photographs, correspondence, drawings, clippings, reports, financial records, awards, journals, articles, and other material pertaining to and deriving from Sanders' editorship of The Breeder's Gazette, his work in livestock breeding, and his authorship of several books on agricultural persons and subjects.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Evan Fay Earle, July 2008Kavitha Reddy, December 2008

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Alvin Howard Sanders (1860-1948) was the son of James Harvey Sanders, a renowned livestock author and founder of the leading livestock journal, The Breeder's Gazette.

Alvin attended Cornell University in Ithaca, New York from 1878 to 1879. His father, then editor of the National Livestock Journal in Chicago called upon his son to help him in the editorial office. In 1881, Alvin graduated from the Union College of Law and received his license from the state of Illinois in 1882.

James Sanders founded The Breeder's Gazette in Chicago on December 1, 1881. Alvin decided to join his father in his new endeavor and was employed on the staff as co-editor. He married Elizabeth A. Hildebrand of Chicago on April 25, 1882. By 1892, Alvin had become editor and owner of The Breeder's Gazette.
On February 20, 1900, the Senate confirmed the appointment of Alvin Sanders as a member of the American delegation to the Paris Exposition.

Atter the Union Stock Yards opened in Chicago, Arthur G. Leonard, Robert Ogilvie, and Alvin Sanders formed the Saddle and Sirloin Club located at the Stockyard Inn in 1903. On the club walls were portraits of the important persons in the livestock movement. In Alvin's book, At the Sign of the Stock Yard Inn, he wrote about the lives of Robert Bakewell, Thomas Bates, Amos Cruickshank and others.

On August 16, 1905, a convention was held at the Illinois Theater in Chicago which was attended by leading commercial and agricultural organizations. It was here that the American Reciprocal Tariff League was formed. Alvin was elected chairman and held this position until 1909. During his service, he corresponded with President Theodore Roosevelt regarding foreign trade agreements with the United States.
President William Taft appointed a Tariff Board in September 1909 which included Henry C. Emery, professor at Yale; James B. Reynolds, Assistant Secretary of Treasury; and Alvin Sanders.

Alvin wrote many livestock books which included such works as A History of Aberdeen- Angus Cattle, Red White and Roan, Short-Horn Cattle, and The Story of the Herefords. In 1917, he published in collaboration with Wayne Dinsmore, A History of the Percheron Horse. The French government awarded him with the Cross of Officer Merite Agricole in appreciation for his work.

Over the years, he was awarded many awards and honors. He received the degree of Doctor of Agriculture from the University of Illinois in 1906. In 1911, he was presented with the Honorary Degree of Doctor of Laws from Kenyon College. Alvin was also awarded the Chevalier of the Order of Leopold from the King of Belgium for his contribution in promoting interest in scientific stock breeding.

He was a member of many different agricultural organizations. He was made president of the Saddle and Sirloin Club in 1915. Governor Frank O. Lowden appointed him a member of the Illinois Board of Agricultural Advisors in 1917.
Alvin Sanders retired from The Breeder's Gazette journal in 1927. The livestock journal continued to be published by different editors until it went out of business in 1964.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Photographs, correspondence, drawings, clippings, reports, financial records, awards, journals, articles, and other material pertaining to and deriving from Sanders' editorship of The Breeder's Gazette, his work in livestock breeding, and his authorship of several books on agricultural persons and subjects.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Alvin Howard Sanders papers, #6312. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Killerby Hall in 1915. Old home of Thomas and John Booth.

scopecontent.p: Robert Bruce home, Ireland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Managers residence on the Locust Valley farm of Russell Grinnell. Robert F. Hildebrand, photographer of Landscapes and animals, NY.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodburn house, Kentucky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Home of Thomas Bates at Kirklevinqton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Robert Collinq home, Barmpton, County Durham.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The big stone stable at Ladykirk. W. Green, Berwick.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mrs. Minnie W. Miller Hampshire sheep, Proprietess Thousand Springs Farm, Wendell, Idaho. Livestock Photo Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: London Central Markets.

did.unittitle: Pasture scene a t L.C. Emmons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pasture scene L.C. Emmons, Swarthmore, PA. H.A. Strohmeyer, Jr., Photographer of animals, NY.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thousand Springs farm, Wendell, Idaho. Guernsey cattle and a few of the springs. Livestock Photo Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Milking cows in village of Carantin, France. Photo by J.E. Wing

scopecontent.p: Interior room at the Saddle and Sirloin Club, Chicago.

scopecontent.p: International Live Stock Exposition building, front exterior view.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indiana pig club boy getting in training for the show. J.G. Allen, West Lafayette, IN.

did.unittitle: Jos. E. Wing and Perry G. Holden

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mr. Joseph E. Wing and Prof. Perry G. Holden discussing their mutually favorite topic, alfalfa. Breeders Gazette (frontispiece).

did.unittitle: Championship cattle bred by T.A. Russell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo & brief history of Flower Girl 18th & Missie of Downsview cattle bred by T.A. Russell, Downsview.

did.unittitle: Barn Sketch by Joseph E. Wing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketch of a barn for feeding 1000 lambs and a written proposal by Joseph E. Wing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of William Burlton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Hon. T.C. Jones, Associate editor of National Livestock Journal. Bodurthas Studio, Sandusky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of James Howie. McGregor Studio, Kilmarnock, Scotland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Late Lord Fitzhardinge.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Warfield, famous Shorthorn breeder who owned Grasmere near Lexington, KY, wrote the first American text on stock breeding. Mullen Studio, Lexington, KY.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of L.N. Bonhan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of A.M. Garland. Brands Studios, Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captain Barclay walking 1000 miles in 1000 hours. Capt. Barclay of Ury, the father of Shorthorn breeding in North Scotland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of J.G. Imbodeu. Vandeventer Studio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of C.A. Lewis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Ashton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Foster Dwight Coburn. Secretary of the Kansas State Board of Agriculture at Topeka. Editor of the Livestock Indicator of Kansas City. Author of the Book of Alfalfa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Geo. C. Morrow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Amos Cruickshank. Famous Scottish breeder of Shorthorn cattle in the 1800's. E. Geering, Aberdeen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Edward N. Wentworth. Director of the Livestock Bureau for Armour & Co. He also served on the Breeder's Gazette staff. Also, served on faculties of Iowa and Kansas State colleges. Author of The Portrait Gallery of the Saddle and Sirloin Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of E.T. Robbins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Hon. James Wilson, Secretary of the US Department of Agriculture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Alvin Howard Sanders. Livestock Journalist, editor of the Breeders Gazette and subject of this collection. P.H. Alusragh, Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Cochrane. McGregor & Sons, Kilmarnock.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of A. S. Alexander. DeTonge, Madison, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of William Arnon Henry. Cornell University, 1880, and Dean of College of Agriculture, University of Wisconsin. S.L. Stein, Milwaukee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Will Barnes. Harris & Ewing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: Armour Portrait Ready for Place in Hall of Fame.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Philip D. Armour. Founder of the Armour Meat Packing Company, Chicago. Brisbois, Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Death of Noted Agriculturist, Mr. Michael Logan of Bargenoch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Michael Logan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earl of Coventry, noted Hereford Breeder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donation of Lord Coventry's photo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address by Alvin H. Sanders, D. Agr. L.L.D., editor of the Breeders Gazette, at the Univ of Illinois, Jan 28, 1914.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Philip D. Armour in the Illinois Farmers' Hall of Fame, University of Illinois.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo of an oil painting of Amos Cruickshank, famous Scottish breeder of Shorthorn Cattle. This painting was done from a photo taken by Alvin Sanders when he called on Mr. Cruickshank in Scotland and is believed to have been the last photo taken of him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eulogy of Mrs. John Clay including her portrait and her only son, John Clay, Jr.

did.unittitle: Mrs. John Clay and John Clay, Jr. portrait

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of "Jack" Clay and his mother, Mrs. John Clay

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Samuel R. Guard. He took over the Breeders Gazette in 1927.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group photo of Alvin Sanders (right), Mrs. Sanders (middle), & Secretary of State, Philander Knox (left).

scopecontent.p: Portrait of Joseph E. Wing, writer for the Breeders Gazette.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Amos Cruickshank for the Breeders Gazette issue of Christmas 1909.
